methods.description: This method describes the process of coating sand or diatomaceous earth with metallic hydroxides using solutions of ferric chloride, magnesium chloride, or aluminum chloride. The purpose is to modify the particles for subsequent experimental use.

steps.description: Mix sand or diatomaceous earth with sufficient solutions of ferric chloride, magnesium chloride, or aluminum chloride to cover it for 30 minutes.
steps.searchable_summary: coat particles with metallic hydroxide solutions

steps.description: Drain excess liquid and allow the solids to air dry.
Mix samples with approximately 2 volumes of 3 N ammonium hydroxide for 10 min, rinse with deionized water, allow to dry, and rinse again.
steps.searchable_summary: treat particles with ammonium hydroxide and rinse

steps.description: Sequentially coat particles by repeating the procedure with different metallic salts.
steps.searchable_summary: apply sequential coatings with different metallic salts

methods.description: This method determines the amount of metal deposited on particles by using hydrochloric acid and analyzing the solution with an ICAP spectrophotometer.

steps.description: Mix samples of sand and diatomaceous earth with 5 volumes of 6 N HCl overnight.
steps.searchable_summary: extract metals from particles using HCl

steps.description: Separate solids from the solution by settling (sand) or by centrifugation at 300 g for 5 min (diatomaceous earth).
Analyze the composition of the solutions using an Inductively Coupled Argon Plasma (ICAP) spectrophotometer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze metal content with ICAP spectrophotometer

methods.description: This method measures the zeta potential of diatomaceous earth particles in a specific buffer using a zeta meter.

steps.description: Measure the zeta potential of diatomaceous earth particles in 0.02 M glycine 0.02 M imidazole buffer using a Lazer Zee model 501 zeta meter.
steps.searchable_summary: measure zeta potential with zeta meter

methods.description: This method involves conducting adsorption studies to determine the adsorption of bacteria and viruses to particles.

steps.description: Mix 2 grams of sand or 0.5 grams of diatomaceous earth in buffer with the appropriate virus or bacterium for 5 min on a reciprocating shaker.
steps.searchable_summary: mix particles with microorganisms for adsorption

steps.description: Assay the supernatant fractions after settling (sand) or after low-speed centrifugation (300 g for 5 min for diatomaceous earth).
Recover adsorbed bacteria and viruses by mixing samples with 20 ml of 3% beef extract, pH 7, for 5 min.
steps.searchable_summary: recover adsorbed microorganisms with beef extract
